title: Collection of Skin Biopsies from Cattle
author: Froylan Sosa
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bm3yk8pw

steps.title: Restrain the animal in a head chute
steps.instruction: Restrain the animal in a head chute.

steps.title: Label the cassettes - make sure...
steps.instruction: Label the cassettes - make sure to use a permanent marker for histology or pencil.

steps.title: Clip the biopsy area with clippers...
steps.instruction: Clip the biopsy area with clippers and wipe down with ethanol-soaked gauze. We typically sample the thorax (middle of 5 or 6th rib) - or shoulder area

steps.title: Wipe area with a sterile dry gauze
steps.instruction: Wipe area with a sterile dry gauze.

steps.title: Spray 5% lidocaine onto the biopsy...
steps.instruction: Spray 5% lidocaine onto the biopsy area and wait for 30 sec.

steps.title: Collect skin sample by rotating the...
steps.instruction: Collect skin sample by rotating the 5 mm skin punch while pressing downwards with mild force.

steps.title: Clean the skin punch by immersing in 70% ethanol
steps.instruction: Clean the skin punch by immersing in 70% ethanol. The skin punch can be used three to four times.

steps.title: Take the skin sample from skin...
steps.instruction: Take the skin sample from skin punch with a needle or tweezers, and place it into a cassette between the sponges. Close the cassettes.

steps.title: Place the cassette into the container...
steps.instruction: Place the cassette into the container with 10% NBF at room temperature.

steps.title: Place sterile gauze on the biopsy...
steps.instruction: Place sterile gauze on the biopsy site to remove excess blood or stop bleeding.

steps.title: Place some super glue into the wound site
steps.instruction: Place some super glue into the wound site. Wait 10 seconds.

steps.title: Spray tick spray around the biopsy site
steps.instruction: Spray tick spray around the biopsy site.

steps.title: To process the biopsy, leave the...
steps.instruction: To process the biopsy, leave the skin samples in NBF for 16-20 h at room temperature. Rinse the skin-containing cassettes in PBS twice, 15 min each time. Cut the skin samples in half and place them back into the cassette (use gloves). Store cassettes in a container containing PBS.
